# Read the file and print it to inspect
path = "/mnt/data/file-DaHEqdNn5NnBDoNWKYBMSf-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

text = open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8").read()

# Helper to add a span dictionary
spans = []

def add_span(start, end, citation_type):
    # Trim whitespace at edges as per rules (shouldn't have whitespace)
    # But ensure we don't strip the content in indices; for safety, adjust indices if there are spaces.
    # We'll move start forward while char is whitespace or quotes or brackets or punctuation to exclude edge marks
    # However we are already computing inside; so likely not needed.
    span_text = text[start:end]
    spans.append({"span_start": start, "span_end": end, "span_text": span_text, "citation_type": citation_type})

# 1. Main body: three quotes.
import re

# Find all spans inside double quotes
quote_spans = [(m.start(), m.end()) for m in re.finditer(r'"', text)]
quote_spans[:10], len(quote_spans)

for i, (s,e) in enumerate(quote_spans):
    context_start = max(0, s-80)
    context_end = min(len(text), e+80)
    print(i, s, e, repr(text[context_start:context_end]))
    print()

import re

# Span 1: First quoted ayah
start1 = quote_spans[0][1]  # 166
end1 = quote_spans[1][0]    # 707
ay1 = text[start1:end1]
ay1[:50], ay1[-50:], (start1, end1)

start2 = quote_spans[2][1]  # 1182
end2 = quote_spans[3][0]    # 1257
frag2 = text[start2:end2]
frag2, (start2, end2)

# Third quote content: from 1413 up to the next newline
start3 = quote_spans[4][1]  # 1413
# find the index of the newline following
nl_index = text.find("\n", start3)
end3 = nl_index if nl_index != -1 else len(text)
frag3 = text[start3:end3]
frag3, (start3, end3)

# Find "وحديث:" occurrence in the text (reference [1])
match_hadith1 = re.search(r'حديث:\s*([^\.\n]+)', text)  # capture up to period or newline
match_hadith1.group(0), match_hadith1.group(1)[:100], match_hadith1.start(), match_hadith1.end()

h1_start = match_hadith1.start(1)
h1_end = match_hadith1.end(1)
text[h1_start:h1_end], (h1_start, h1_end)

# Find "قال تعالى:" after first hadith in reference [1]
start_pos = match_hadith1.end()
m_qa1 = re.search(r'قال تعالى:\s*', text[start_pos:])
if m_qa1:
    qa1_prefix_start = start_pos + m_qa1.start()
    qa1_content_start = start_pos + m_qa1.end()
    # content continues until a left brace '{' which precedes the surah reference.
    brace_idx = text.find('{', qa1_content_start)
    # but ensure to not include trailing spaces before brace
    content_end = brace_idx if brace_idx != -1 else None
    snippet = text[qa1_content_start:content_end]
    # strip trailing whitespace but we must adjust indices accordingly
    # get right-stripped length difference
    rstrip_len = len(snippet) - len(snippet.rstrip())
    content_end_adj = content_end - rstrip_len
    # also strip leading whitespace (though likely none)
    lstrip_len = len(snippet) - len(snippet.lstrip())
    qa1_content_start += lstrip_len
    snippet_final = text[qa1_content_start:content_end_adj]
    print((qa1_content_start, content_end_adj))
    print(snippet_final[:80], '...', snippet_final[-80:])
else:
    print("Not found")

# Hadith in [2] in quotes
start4 = quote_spans[5][1]
end4 = quote_spans[6][0]
hadith2 = text[start4:end4]
(start4, end4), hadith2

m_par = re.search(r'قال تعالى:\s*\(', text)
m_par.start(), m_par.end(), text[m_par.start():m_par.start()+30]

open_par_content_start = m_par.end()
close_par_idx = text.find(')', open_par_content_start)
open_par_content_start, close_par_idx, text[open_par_content_start:open_par_content_start+30]

first_chars = [(i, ch, ord(ch)) for i, ch in enumerate(ayah_par[:5])]
last_chars = [(len(ayah_par)-5+i, ch, ord(ch)) for i, ch in enumerate(ayah_par[-5:])]
first_chars, last_chars

spans = []
# Add each computed span
add_span(start1, end1, "Aya")
add_span(start2, end2, "Aya")
add_span(start3, end3, "Aya")
add_span(h1_start, h1_end, "Hadeeth")
add_span(1911, 2454, "Aya")
add_span(start4, end4, "Hadeeth")
add_span(open_par_content_start, close_par_idx, "Aya")

# Prepare JSON object
result = {"citations": spans}
result
